_README: 1. Open your CSV/Parquet file and note the exact column names.
2. Fill in 'your_column' with the name as it appears in YOUR file.
4. Open the dashboard in a browser (python -m http.server 8000).
columns_essential  → columns the dashboard NEEDS to work
columns_sidebar    → columns used to filter/group bonds (left panel)
columns_extra      → extra info columns shown in the bottom table
chart_axes         → which numeric columns can be used as X/Y axes

_help: These columns are REQUIRED for the dashboard to work.
Set 'your_column' to the exact name in your dataset.
DO NOT change 'dashboard_key' — the dashboard needs these exact names.

bond_id.description: Unique bond identifier (e.g. ISIN, CUSIP, bond ID)

spread.description: Bond spread in basis points (OAS, ASW, Z-spread…)

maturity_date.description: Bond maturity date (any date format: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY…)

snapshot_date.description: Date of the data snapshot / observation

_help: These columns appear on the LEFT SIDE of the dashboard as filters.
They let you group and select bonds (e.g. by issuer, bond type, date).
The ORDER matters: first = main search, middle = pill filters, last = date list.
Each entry needs:
your_column   → exact column name in your dataset
label         → what the user sees in the dashboard
style         → 'search' (searchable list), 'pills' (clickable buttons), or 'datelist'
Optional:
default       → default value (e.g. 'All' to show all bond types)
show_all      → true/false, add an 'All' option that shows everything
These columns appear ONLY in the bottom table for extra bond info.
Add as many as you want — they won't affect the chart or filters.
Just list the exact column names from your dataset.
These columns can be selected as X or Y axis on the chart.
TTM (time to maturity) is always available — no need to add it.
The spread column from 'columns_essential' is automatically included too.
For each axis, set:
your_column  → exact column name in your dataset
label        → display name on the chart
default_x    → true if this should be the default X axis
default_y    → true if this should be the default Y axis
Optional: override how column names appear in the dashboard.
Format:  'internal_key': 'Display Label'
If not set, the dashboard will auto-generate labels from column names.
